target.name: Altos Labs
target.founder: Hal Barron, Rick Klausner, Hans Bishop

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Late Stage → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $3.0B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Biotech & Health (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 16 same-sector peers exist in Biotech & Health, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Altos Labs is a late-stage biotech company that has raised $3.0B to date across multiple funding rounds. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 11 peers in Biotech & Health (3 public, 8 private), including Tempus AI, Abridge, and Ultima Genomics.

Only one valuation method proved applicable with sufficient data. Stage-Progression anchoring uses the $3.0B total raised as a proxy baseline and applies a 1.2x step-up multiplier typical of late-stage to next-stage progression, yielding $3.6B. Peer-Median triangulation was not viable due to insufficient narrow-stage comparables (0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage peers meeting data thresholds; the broader sector contains 16 biotech peers but none clustered tightly enough to avoid distortion). Public-Comp Anchoring, while supported by 3 public biotech tickers trading at a 6.6x median P/S, could not be applied—private companies including Altos Labs do not publicly disclose revenue.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $3.1B to $4.1B, with a median of $3.6B. This range reflects stage-typical growth expectations applied to deployed capital. Key uncertainties include the absence of revenue disclosure, lack of narrowly comparable late-stage private biotech valuations, and sensitivity to unrealized internal milestones. The single-method result warrants caution; broader market conditions and company-specific clinical progress remain material drivers not captured in this quantitative exercise.
